content.boundary: Applies nationally to all villages under 2,000 inhabitants with a primary school of any operating model (standalone, networked, grade-split, church-run, or consolidated); the registry and thresholds apply as a reporting/transparency requirement, not as a mandate to adopt any particular structural model.
content.change_lever: Create a standing national infrastructure — a public registry of closure/retention decisions and post-decision outcomes (enrollment, cost, academic, demographic), transparent minimum-viability and cost-accounting standards, and standardized teacher-training/support criteria — that all tankerület decisions must reference and report against, functioning as an accountability and learning layer over whichever operational model (T1-T4) is chosen locally.
content.name: National transparency and evidence infrastructure: viability thresholds, cost-visibility, and a closure/outcome registry
content.system_problem: Hungary's school-network decisions currently lack any standing feedback mechanism connecting demographic data, cost data, and outcome evidence; historical policy swings (1950s centralization, 2004-2008 amalgamation wave, 2013 KLIK centralization, 2017 re-decentralization) were driven by top-down political decisions rather than institutionalized, transparent evidence, and independent studies (e.g., cost-savings claims) are rarely verified after the fact.
